title: symphyotrichum drummondii var. parviceps
intro: Symphyotrichum drummondii var. parviceps, also known as Drummond's aster, is a perennial plant species within the Asteraceae family, characterized by its small, white or pinkish flowers and narrow leaves. Traditionally, Drummond's aster has been used for its medicinal properties, particularly its anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial effects. The plant has been used to treat various health issues, including fever, colds, and respiratory problems, as well as to aid in wound healing and reduce pain. In herbal preparations, the plant is often used as a tea, tincture, or infused oil, and is valued for its potential to boost the immune system and promote overall well-being.
